_readme: The GENERIC base layer: the default knob values every render inherits (resolution: CLI > recipe.json > THIS FILE). These are starting points, not a universal look - a value with no cross-data technical reason is a TUNED DEFAULT, marked as such in its 'why' note and re-derived per data class through a one-knob ladder. A change here restyles every dataset whose recipe does not pin the knob (approved recipes pin ALL knobs and are immune) and lands as a declared delta through the no-regression sweep. starcomb hard-fails if this file and its knob schema drift apart.

why.starless_target: The sky background's display level. Too high lifts the estimator's rim residual into visibility; the low target keeps the sky dark where that residual lives. Tuned default.
why.stretch_mode: mtf = linked autostretch alone. ghs = autostretch + an autoghs FINISHING pass: a single midtone transfer mis-serves both ends of high-dynamic-range deep data (the bright-core structure/grain ratio collapses and the faint end lands far below a good allocation), and a single autoghs from linear cannot replace it (its gain concentrates at the sky pedestal, not the object). Generic stays mtf; ghs is a per-class look adopted only on user approval.
why.ghs_sp_k: GHS symmetry point = stretched-sky median + k*sigma. k sets how far above the sky the toe sits; higher k pins more of the low end as sky. Tuned default (ghs path only).
why.ghs_amount: GHS stretch strength D. Too low under-lifts; too high mostly raises the sky pedestal at equal focus. Tuned default (ghs path).
why.ghs_focus: GHS focus b: a high b is so symmetry-point-focused the object above the SP gets almost nothing; a low b spreads the gain across the object's mid-tones. Tuned default (ghs path).
why.starless_denoise: vstpost = a post-stretch VST denoise at half strength - a self-flat adaptation, because on self-flat data the noise is radial after V(r) division so any LINEAR denoise placement imprints a radial signature. CLASS LIMIT: a post-stretch VST crushes object chroma whose contrast sits near the grain scale while strong chroma passes untouched, so on a colour-rich moderate-contrast target ladder this knob first (often 'off'); 'gx' (linear GraXpert denoise) can tint the sky on that class. 'off'/'gx'/'vst' stay recipe-expressible per class.
why.sep_prom: starsep detection prominence in sigma. Above a few sigma the residual is equal - the faint tail below it is noise-level clumping, not separable stars. Tuned default.
why.sep_engine: auto = StarNet2 when the weights are installed, else the mask+inpaint fallback. An engine pin is a per-dataset measurement (e.g. an ultra-wide MW field where the net's residual large-scale structure fails the gate).
why.stretch_linked: The channel coupling of siril's autostretch. auto = linked (the in-house chain serves the broadband/mono class: one calibrated scene, one transfer). linked is the broadband standard; unlinked per-channel differentially amplifies channel noise (the rainbow engine) and after SPCC has no cast left to compensate — a measurement rung only. Narrowband-palette colour+develop is Nightlight's job (render_engine routes an SHO composition to nightlight_sho.py), NOT an in-house stretch mode.
why.satu: Final saturation gain, applied by siril `satu` (background_factor 0 = all pixels, all hues) on the combined render. Saturation scales all colour including star-edge fringe, so it is kept low to avoid amplifying the fringe. Tuned default.
why.cull_pct: Culls the faintest fraction of detections (noise-level clumping) before the star MTF. The faint-field character is set by the star MTF anchor + the starless floor, not the cull, so the exact value is not critical.
why.stars_peak: The MTF anchor level for the star layer's median top-tier star, on the G basis. The anchor POPULATION (top-tier of the star catalog) is inherently frame/dataset-dependent, so the level is not a cross-field constant. Tuned default.
why.stars_anchor: catalog = anchor on the top-tier star population (per-field level); 'noise' = k*sigma_G, which holds a physical star's brightness across rebuilds of ONE field but requires a per-dataset k (k restates that field's star statistics and has no cross-field value).
why.stars_floor: Floors the star layer at this many sigma to kill the ghost aura: the stars-layer skirt annulus amplifies star-subtraction noise through the MTF's very large low-end gain, and flooring cuts that wing. Mechanism is class-general (it removes only sub-noise signal). Tuned default.
why.black_point: A linear black-point shift on the starless output floor, driven by siril `mtf b 0.5 1` (midtones 0.5 = identity, low shadow clip at b): it moves the floor while preserving contrast and all differences. Set to place the sky floor just above clip. Class risk: too high crushes faint extended signal on deeper data. Tuned default.
why.jpg_quality: q100 + 4:4:4: q92 with 4:2:0 chroma subsampling halves chroma resolution and adds star-edge ringing (a pixeled aura). Finals also export lossless PNGs.
why.noise_anchor_k: No generic value exists by construction: k restates one field's star statistics, so a value from one field is a large brightness error on another. Per-dataset recipes only.
why.bgelin_mode: gx = the standard full background extraction (GraXpert AI BGE + subsky 1), run star-ful so the frame-filling Milky Way is not read as background and absorbed; the CLI is AI-mode-only (smoothing 0.0, Subtraction). CLASS LIMIT: the AI cannot distinguish frame-filling FAINT nebulosity from a sky gradient and absorbs it as background (bright compact objects saturate out of its input clip and survive). Smoothing is NOT a protection lever (it blurs the model output, not the inference). plane = subsky 1 only: removes the gate's gradient class but cannot absorb localized nebulosity by construction - the retention mode for fields that ARE mostly object. off = passthrough (measurement).
